title: Classes, Modifiers, Private State, and Abstract Contracts · Narrowing Workshop
summary: Implement scheduling components with runtime invariants, replaceable policies, and explicit construction rules. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a typed scheduling component library while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish instance-side and static-side class types, fields, methods, and constructor signatures.
Use public, protected, TypeScript private, and JavaScript hash-private fields with their actual compile-time and runtime boundaries.
Use parameter properties only when concise construction does not hide validation or ownership transitions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
